summary: Both Round-1 majors are honestly closed and all four addressed minors hold up to the litmus test. The two new HomeViewModelTest cases for corruptionOccurred pin both polarities so a constant-return regression on either side would fail. The buildAndWrite pair proves (a) buildExport is called with ExportSource.About, (b) writeTo receives the SAF Uri, (c) writeTo's content argument equals buildExport's return (so a stale/empty-write regression flips assertEquals), and (d) IOException is captured as Result.failure with the original exception preserved. RecordingLogExport goes through the LogExportManager(context) secondary ctor exactly as the :crash process does — and because both suspend methods are overridden, the inherited openOutputStreamForTest and real DeviceInfoCollector/LogcatReader are never exercised, so there is no interference. The ChatViewModelTest minor fixes (currentModelId pin on Draft, Initializing branch test, two-call loadModel assertion) each bite on the corresponding regression, and FakeWarmupCoordinator's ownJob is cancelled in @After via shutdown(). No regressions introduced; no new findings worth flagging.
